The silver-haired elder frowned, but held his temper in check and repeated again, “Han Fengqiu!”

He couldn’t help but raise his voice, though.

“Han-something-qiu?” Zu An looked confused, as though he really couldn’t remember.

The silver-haired elder bubbled with rage.

“Damned brat, were you making fun of me?!”

You have successfully trolled Han Fengqiu for 315… 315… 315…

He refused to believe that a cultivator’s memory could be that bad. This kid was clearly messing with him!

Zu An smiled innocently. “It took you this long to realize this? You don’t seem all that sharp.”

The silver-haired elder couldn’t believe his ears.

You have successfully trolled Han Fengqiu for 999… 999… 999…

A laugh came from within the distant carriage, but it cut off suddenly, almost as if whoever had laughed had immediately covered her mouth with her hand.

The battle between Zu An and the young heir had been exciting and full of surprises, so no one had paid attention to the conversation between the woman and her driver.

In this moment of brief silence, though, her laughter stood out.

Everyone looked towards the source of this abrupt sound. How could there exist in this world someone with such a perfect voice?

However, this fleeting thought was quickly replaced by more important considerations.

Zu An was curious. Hm? This voice does sound rather pleasing. I wonder what the woman herself looks like… The fact that she’s still here means she at least has some loyalty. I supposed I didn’t save her life for nothing.

The silver-haired elder was more concerned. I can’t discern that driver’s cultivation. Is he an ordinary person or a powerful cultivator? There’s no way an ordinary person would stay here and watch all this unfolding as though watching a play, right?

Murong Qinghe spoke quietly to Chu Youzhao. “Big brother Chu, your brother-in-law is too reckless! He isn’t strong enough, yet he’s provoked another powerful expert. I can’t tell if he’s smart or not.”

As the young miss of the Murong clan, she obviously knew who Han Fengqiu was, and she knew his cultivation level as well. Someone at the peak of the ninth rank could overwhelm anyone below the master rank!

She wasn’t so concerned about Zu An’s intelligence, though. She was more worried that this might cause trouble for her big brother Chu.

She was still concerned by how Chu Youzhao had run out to protect Zu An earlier on, and didn’t want to go through that again.

“My eldest sister said that brother-in-law looks uncouth and impetuous, but he actually had an incredibly sharp mind,” explained Chu Youzhao. “He’ll always leave his enemies seething in anger, while losing nothing in return. Even though this doesn’t really conform to the virtues that we are taught, it’s still pretty fun to watch sometimes.”

When she’d told her about this, her big sister’s eyes had been shining brightly. Chu Youzhao did not really take her words to heart, though. It felt like her eldest sister was merely describing a shameless and despicable man.

Having spent some time around this brother-in-law, though, she gradually understood what her big sis meant.

Her brother-in-law was really good at pissing people off. King Qi’s heir had experienced it, and now, this elder was going to suffer the same fate.

His great comeback had left her eyes sparkling as well. She was now full of confidence in her brother-in-law.

Han Fengqiu looked away from the carriage. It looked too ordinary to belong to anyone of significant strength, even if they had some skills. Rescuing King Qi’s heir was the most important thing right now.

He looked coldly at Zu An. “Kid, you’ve successfully made me angry.”

Zu An sighed. “Why do all you pompous pricks always sound the same? Do you think that you’re some powerful CEO or something?”

King Qi’s heir was in so much pain that he was about to cry. Even though Zu An’s words filled him with all manner of curses he wished to unload, he still didn’t dare to speak.

This guy really is a madman, so it’s not worth picking a fight with him. He would take his time to exact revenge once Uncle Han saved him.

Han Fengqiu’s eyes narrowed. Even though he didn’t know what a CEO was, the ridicule in his voice was obvious. “Brat, you’ll soon pay dearly for those reckless words.”

“Did you ask me to move my foot just now?” asked Zu An coldly.

Han Fengqiu nodded his head slightly. “Indeed. Move it, or else this old one…”

Before he could finish speaking, Zu An had already stomped his foot down. The sound of bones shattering was accompanied by the young heir’s squealing, horrifying the onlookers.

Chu Youzhao was stupefied. No matter how good her opinion of her brother-in-law was becoming, he had surely done it this time.

Murong Qinghe looked at Zu An as though looking at a dead person. Shattering the young heir’s knee in front of an expert at the peak of the ninth rank… This man is beyond saving. He was merely waiting for death.

The driver with the missing tooth gasped. “I thought that he was the slick and cunning sort. I didn’t expect him to be this tough and stubborn! This kid is quite frightening!”

The woman in the carriage frowned, but refrained from comment.

Han Fengqiu was so angry that his entire body was shaking “How dare you…?!”

You have successfully trolled Han Fengqiu for 998… 998… 998…

Zu An fixed him with a disdainful look. “You’re right. Sometimes, you really do pay dearly for speaking reckless words. If you had spoken a little more nicely, I might have spared him, but you decided to speak to me the way you did, as though you were almost afraid that I would end up not doing something to him. You know, I’m really starting to wonder if you’re a spy in King Qi’s manor who’s trying to use this as an opportunity to get rid of his heir.”

Han Fengqiu almost choked on his breath. He’d sensed a sudden spike of alarm, afraid that King Qi might be misled.

However, he quickly calmed down. “You have a really sharp tongue. Unfortunately, talk is useless in this world!”

His longsword left his waist as soon as he finished his sentence, sending out an incredibly sharp streak of sword ki, which locked onto Zu An.

Zu An felt goosebumps rising all over his body. He quickly raised the Tai’e Sword to protect himself.

With a bang, he was blasted back several zhang of distance. Blood dripped from between his fingers.

Han Fengqiu was already standing in front of King Qi’s heir. He squatted down to take a look. “My Lord, are you okay?”

The young heir in question raised his hand and pointed at Zu An. He said through gritted teeth, “I won’t die. Break every bone in that bastard’s body first. Don’t leave a single one intact!”

When had he ever been injured so badly in his lifetime? When had he ever experienced such humiliation? There was only one thought in his head right now, which was revenge. He was going to make Zu An regret being born into this world.

“Understood!” Han Fengqiu stood up, and he instantly locked his aura onto Zu An.

Zu An smiled. “Young heir, have you forgotten the oath you took before the battle? This was supposed to be a fair battle between us. If you turn your clan’s power against me, you might just suffer heaven’s wrath.”

Han Fengqiu frowned. If the young heir had truly taken such an oath, he really couldn’t interfere.

Unexpectedly, King Qi’s heir pointed towards the ice sculptures littering the street, “I may have said that I wouldn’t bring the strength of King Qi Estate against you to exact revenge. However, it seems you’ve killed many court officials. This is a crime punishable by death! Uncle Han would only be following the law by bringing you to justice.”

chapters.title: Chapter 691: Fury
chapters.body:
Chapter 691: Fury

Zu An gasped in amazement. “I thought I was already shameless enough, but it seems I’m a novice compared to you! You’re actually using the law to exact revenge on me.”

King Qi’s heir struggled to a seated position. He tried his best to assume his usual proud demeanor, but being struck by lightning left him looking rather hideous, no matter how he tried to hide it.  “Don’t misunderstand me. I am not trying to exact revenge. After all, I made an oath, bound by the natural laws of the world. How could I go against that?”

He didn’t dare underestimate the power of these oaths, and he was doing his best to avoid suffering the consequences of breaking this one.

Zu An sneered. “Have you heard this phrase before? ‘You shouldn’t deceive the heavens.’”

King Qi’s heir roared with laughter. “That is merely something the weak use to console themselves. The heavens are heartless, and view all men like dogs. The heavenly law does not recognize good and evil. It only requires you to follow its rules. Do that, and you will be safe.”

“This guy is twisting logic!” Chu Youzhao said unhappily. He was clearly still out for revenge, despite his earlier oath. Despicable.

Murong Qinghe frowned as well. As an obsessed martial artist, she believed that a loss was a loss. King Qi’s heir was acting rather shamelessly.

However, as a daughter of the Murong clan, she admired his decision. It would be worrisome if King Qi’s heir was naive.

“The weak?” Zu An’s mocking laughter echoed down the street. “Who was it that was being trampled on like a dead dog just now? Who was the weak?”

The young heir felt his breath catch in his throat. His chest began to heave intensely. What happened earlier on had been utterly humiliating.

You have successfully trolled Zhao Zhi for 999… 999… 999…

Han Fengqiu said with a frown, “My Lord Heir, there’s no need for you to lower yourself to that good-for-nothing’s level.”

King Qi’s heir took a deep breath. He wasn’t a useless and utter fool of a son. He reacted quickly, and said, “Indeed, one’s strength is not only reflected in their cultivation, but also in the resources at his disposal. When comparing the two of us, merely calling you weak is already high praise.”

Zu An laughed out loud. “Who was it that was boasting earlier on that you didn’t rely on anyone but yourself?”

King Qi’s heir was speechless.

Did this guy not know how to have a proper conversation?

He knew that there was no way he would win through verbal sparring. Boiling with shame and fury, he roared, “Uncle Han, cripple him!”

“Very well. My Lord Heir, rest here. Watch the way I deal with this ant.” Han Fengqiu turned around and looked at Zu An. “Brat, you should be honored that this old one is getting personally involved. Your life has finally been made worthy.”

Zu An spat on the ground and said mockingly, “You? A mere ninth rank cultivator? Perhaps you don’t know that I’m constantly in the company of earth immortals and grandmasters? Several master rank cultivators have died at my hands. You’re just a pathetic ninth rank cultivator. I really don’t know where you get your confidence from.”

Both Mosquito Daoist and Old Mi had both died at his hands. Furthermore, the souls of Mi Li, Qin Shihuang, and the final Shang king Wu Geng, were all beings that were on par with the current emperor. There weren’t that many truly powerful cultivators who could compare to him, and so he obviously didn’t view someone at the peak of the ninth rank to be worthy.

However, since there was no way anyone else could have known about this, they all thought that he was spouting a load of bullshit.

Murong Qinghe scoffed. “Big brother Chu, your brother-in-law’s cultivation is pretty decent, but he really takes his boasting too far.”

Chu Youzhao felt her pretty face begin to warm. Even though she knew that her brother-in-law was formidable, comparing himself to earth immortals and grandmasters was just too much. Even she was feeling embarrassed for him.

A distance away, the driver chuckled. “He’s killed a few masters? Madam, this friend of yours really is quite bold.”

He received no reply.

Han Fengqiu obviously didn’t believe him either. “You are so young, yet you are full of nonsense. Today, this old one will properly discipline you on behalf of your family. You will learn that you can stuff whatever food you want into your mouth, but you cannot use it to idly spew words…”

Before he could finish, Zu An interrupted him. “Your pants have dropped.”

Han Fengqiu was momentarily stunned, then he felt a chill across his legs. When he looked down, his pants were bunched around his ankles.

His shriveled manhood was quivering in the cold wind, looking extremely pitiful.

A moment ago, he had been speaking in such a domineering fashion, yet all of his swagger disappeared in an instant.

“Ah!!”

Chu Youzhao and Murong Qinghe both covered their eyes and turned around, their faces bright red.

What the heck is going on? We’ve never seen any of this in all our years alive. Why are we being subjected to that dirty sight so many times today?

Chu Youzhao couldn’t help but remember her brother-in-law. Why are other men so much smaller than him?

This was true for King Qi’s heir, and now, it was true for this ninth-ranked expert as well.

King Qi’s heir did not share her thoughts. In fact, even though Han Fengqiu was on his side, the young master almost couldn’t contain his laughter.

He gave it another look, and a big smile spread across his face. He finally regained his confidence.

In the future, he could tell others that he was better than a ninth-ranked expert… No, I can’t. Uncle Han might kill me for that.

Unlike King Qi’s heir, who was holding back his laughter with great difficulty, the driver with the missing tooth let his own laughter ring out in loud peals. “So this fellow likes to walk around with his balls dangling! How old are you already? Why are you still doing that? Hahaha, you’re killing me.”

The madam inside the carriage was speechless.

She shut the curtains in front of her completely. Such a scene was just too offensive to the eyes.

Han Fengqiu’s mind went completely blank. He was already considered an elder, and had reached the peak of the ninth rank. He thought he’d been through every conceivable situation.

However, he had never experienced such a situation before.

He finally snapped out of his daze when he heard the laughter ringing all around him. He pulled up his pants as he charged at Zu An: “Die!”

You have successfully trolled Han Fengqiu for 1024… 1024… 1024…

His rage knew now bounds. His eye sockets felt like they were splitting, and he was burning with a frenzy of hatred. He lost any semblance of being a high-class elder.

He did not think that Zu An had anything to do with his pants dropping. He was just extremely put off by the kid’s vicious tongue. He was going to rip his face apart and feed his tongue to the dogs in order to cool off.

As for the driver with the missing tooth, he would make sure that he wouldn’t be able to laugh anymore either.

A ninth rank expert moved extremely quickly. He shot forward like a streak of lightning, and neither Murong Qinghe nor Chu Youzhao could react.

However, Zu An was already prepared, dodging aside with the help of his Sunflower Phantasm. He’d already gone through Mi Li’s rigorous training, and he’d even managed to dodge some of her strikes. What could a mere ninth rank, who only reached his rank because of his seniority, do to him?

“Huh?” The driver with the missing tooth had been thoroughly enjoying himself, but this development shook him to his core. Zu An’s grasp of timing was just too perfect.

Dodging the attacks of the young heir wasn’t anything to brag about, but evading the strikes of someone at the peak of ninth rank was a completely different matter. They could lock onto their target’s auras, which rendered ordinary movement techniques meaningless. However, Zu An still managed to dodge it!

Is this really a coincidence, or…

Han Fengqiu wasn’t in the mood to notice this. There was only one thought in his mind right now—he was going to catch Zu An, and then kill him.

Zu An was feeling the pressure as well. Han Fengqiu was probably exceeding his usual strength by twenty percent, so his speed was far greater than Zhao Zhi’s. If not for his mastery of his Sunflower Phantasm, he might have been caught already. Even so, this was a dangerous fight.

He was tempted to say ‘your pants have dropped again’, but he didn’t do it. Even though the skill ‘Keyboard, Come!’ was powerful, it wasn’t without cost. The backlash of causing his opponent’s pants to drop once shouldn’t be too bad, but if he did it repeatedly, it might overwhelm him.

Han Fengqiu was beginning to feel anxious. He was a powerful cultivator at the peak of the ninth rank, yet he couldn’t take out his opponent despite going all out. This fellow was as slippery as a loach.

It was all because of the damned restrictions around the airspace of the capital. If he could utilise his ability to fly and attack from above, all of Zu An’s speed would be nullified.

chapters.title: Chapter 692: The Madam’S Invitation
chapters.body:
Chapter 692: The Madam’S Invitation

King Qi’s heir was waiting for his Uncle Han to take down his opponent, so that he could take his revenge.

Unexpectedly, even after such a long time, Han Fengqiu was still unable to subdue his opponent.

He felt an odd sense of relief. Look, not even someone at the peak of the ninth rank can subdue him. Of course an eighth-ranked cultivator like me would be defeated.

Murong Qinghe stared at the battle playing out before her, her fierce brows knit together tightly. “Your brother-in-law is really strange… He was fighting toe-to-toe against King Qi’s heir earlier, and he’s now doing the same thing with a cultivator at the peak of the ninth rank. Does he even have a limit?”

“My brother-in-law is really strong, isn’t he?” Chu Youzhao said proudly.

She felt slightly regretful that her eldest sister wasn’t here to witness this.

Han Fengqiu heard the discussions going on around him and stopped, his face burning up with embarrassment.

He felt completely humiliated that he had yet to take down the brat in front of him, despite his cultivation.

He knew that he would not be able to catch Zu An if he continued on in this manner, so he stopped. “You little bastard, you’ve really angered this old one this time.”

Zu An couldn’t help but smile. “Oh? So you weren’t actually that angry when your pants dropped in full view of everyone here.”

He’d danced on the line between life and death while battling more powerful opponents before. No matter how formidable Han Fengqiu was, it wasn’t enough to truly scare him.

Han Fengqiu’s face twitched, then he sighed. Without another word, he drew his sword and held it in front of him.

With an audible hum, the sword began to vibrate.

With his right hand, he drew a large circle in front of him.

The sword in front of him suddenly multiplied, its copies spreading outwards like a peacock’s tail, then blossoming like a chrysanthemum.

Zu An lost his smile as well. Even from a distance away, he could sense the viciousness of this sword ki.

Han Fengqiu flung the ring of swords into the sky. The swords continued to spin and vibrate, filling the air with their humming.

One became two, two became four, four became eight…

Endless sword ki filled the sky.

Han Fengqiu sneered. “Brat, where will you run to now?!”

He and King Qi’s heir both had the same idea. Since he couldn’t deal with his movement technique, he would use an area of effect skill, leaving Zu An no way to escape.

The woman in the carriage spoke up. “Save him!”

In her eyes, there was no way Zu An could avoid this attack.

“Fine!” The driver with the missing tooth cracked a grin and took a single step, appearing right in the middle of the battlefield.

All the sword ki descended in an instant, in an impressive rain of swords. Everything within a range of several dozen li was completely covered. There was nowhere for Zu An to dodge even if he wanted to.

Zu An’s expression grew strange. You’re using a sword? I just happen to have a similar skill.

He was just about to make his move when someone appeared in front of him.

“Hm? The carriage driver?” Zu An recognized who it was right away. This old fellow’s teeth were crooked, and he was even missing one. It was difficult for him to forget such a level of ugliness even if he tried.

The driver with the missing tooth reached upwards with his hand and clenched his fist. It wrapped perfectly around Han Fengqiu’s sword.

When the sword was caught, all the sword energy seemed to evaporate into thin air, immediately dissipating into nothingness.

Han Fengqiu’s smile froze instantly. He was waiting to see Zu An scream in agony from the sword ki. How could he have anticipated such a development?

Despite this, he reacted quickly, immediately offering a respectful greeting to the driver with the missing tooth. “Esteemed senior, Han Fengqiu of King Qi’s Estate offers you his greetings!”

Even though the other party looked slightly younger than himself, in the world of cultivation, seniority resided with the stronger.

Since this man had easily seen through his sword formation and grabbed his sword, he had to be at least a rank above himself. This implied that he was at least a master rank cultivator. Only those who could cultivate their soul would be able to see through the formations that easily, and instantly find its core.

This greeting also helped to re-establish his identity. No matter how powerful this opponent was, he couldn’t possibly be stronger than King Qi, so this also served as a warning.

The driver with the missing tooth huffed angrily. “Don’t try to use King Qi’s name to scare me. Although I could never defeat King Qi, giving you a good beating isn’t that difficult.”

Han Fengqiu could not formulate a reply.

“Oh, right. I was the one who was laughing earlier on. Didn’t you want to beat me up?” The driver smiled ambiguously at Han Fengqiu. He wasn’t planning to let this matter drop. 

Han Fengqiu immediately apologized. “This junior doesn’t dare!”

Fine beads of sweat were already starting to form on his forehead. What the hell is going on? He was running into one freak after another today!

He wanted to completely dominate this situation, and then use this chance to show off a bit. Yet before he even regained some of his honor, he was humiliated again!

“I didn’t think you would.” The driver with the missing tooth said with a snort. He turned to Zu An. “Kid, you’re all right. Your tongue is a little too rough, but you don’t look that bad, and you have a decent cultivation.”

Zu An was speechless. Why are you looking at me like I’m your son-in-law or something?

He quickly clasped his fist and said, “I wouldn't have made such a fool of myself if I had known that senior possessed such powerful cultivation.”

No wonder this man hadn’t thanked him at all when he stopped that horse. The horse never had a chance of harming them in the first place.

“It’s rare to see someone with such chivalry.” The driver with the missing tooth found him more and more pleasing.

Chu Youzhao was overjoyed. Just a moment ago, Zu An had been in danger of being engulfed by endless amounts of sword ki, and she’d been so anxious that her heart had almost leaped out of her chest. Yet, in the next instant, this mysterious expert had stepped in to help her brother-in-law! Judging from his tone, it looked like her brother-in-law was definitely going to be just fine.

She couldn’t help but turn to Murong Qinghe and asked in a hushed tone, “You know the most about cultivators. Do you know who this senior is?”

Murong Qinghe was similarly baffled. “I don’t.”

She knew almost all of the master rank cultivators in the capital, but not a single one of their descriptions matched this mysterious man’s appearance.

The carriage had no clan emblems, and it looked no different from a common carriage. 

A few feet away, King Qi’s heir was sulking.

Han Fengqiu may have been uncertain about facing down this man, but he did not share this uncertainty.

Aside from the emperor himself, there were few in the capital that he needed to fear. “Who are you, distinguished sir? Do you know that shielding a criminal who has murdered military officers is a serious crime? You are stopping the imperial court from dispensing the law!”

The driver with the missing tooth chuckled, revealing two rows of crooked yellow teeth. “You shouldn’t try to scare me with that. I’m sure the imperial court is fully capable of determining truth from falsehood.”

Zu An spoke up as well. “Hey, tomato face, you can stuff anything you want into your mouth, but you can’t just say anything you want. When did I murder any court officials? These fellows were only frozen.”

Zu An retracted the cold energy, and the previously-frozen individuals all regained their consciousness, although many of them were missing arms and legs.

King Qi’s heir was shocked. What kind of move is this? How is such a thing possible? He did not pursue this, though, and continued, “Even if they’re not dead, they’ve lost their limbs because of you. Attacking a military officer is a terrible crime!”

Zu An laughed. “There are so many witnesses here. Clearly, in your desperation to show off, you whipped up such a ruckus, breaking off the large chunks of ice which were their arms and legs. You are obviously the main culprit! Elder whose pants dropped earlier on, please capture this man!”

“You!” King Qi’s heir was shaking all over in anger. He was accustomed to using his identity to deliberately flip the truth around, yet now, he suddenly felt like their identities were flipped.

You have successfully trolled Zhao Zhi for 888… 888… 888…

Han Fengqiu’s eyelids twitched when he heard the words ‘pants dropped’. Was this kid born to piss people off?

You have successfully trolled Han Fengqiu for 999… 999… 999…

He took a deep breath and forced himself to resist the urge to attack. He looked at the driver with the missing tooth. “May I ask your name, honored senior?”

He knew that this mysterious expert was the key to this entire situation. If this man wasn’t here, Zu An would merely be livestock waiting to be slaughtered.

The driver casually waved a hand. “I’ve forgotten my name. I am merely an ordinary carriage driver now.” 

Everyone else was baffled, but Han Fengqiu’s expression flickered. “Could you be…”

However, King Qi’s heir had grown annoyed. “Who the hell cares who he is? Today…”

Han Fengqiu quickly stopped him and whispered in his ear, “My Lord Heir, he is…”

King Qi’s heir immediately began to breathe heavily. He quickly looked towards the ordinary carriage, his eyes full of expectation and desire.

Zu An frowned. What was going on with this fellow? Why did he look like a pig in heat? Wasn’t he afraid that his little one would break off if he got too excited?

The driver with the missing tooth smiled and said to Zu An, “Kid, my Madam invites you for a meeting.”

chapters.title: Chapter 693: Rebound
chapters.body:
Chapter 693: Rebound

When he heard the driver’s invitation to Zu An, the young heir Zhao Zhi’s breathing became hurried.

Few people in this world would be offered an invitation by that Madam.

He was a vain man, but had never had the chance of meeting her in person. He saw her as someone that was beyond the reach of even the emperor, his father, or the heirs of various noble houses, and therefore, it was completely natural for her to disregard him as well.

Yet she’d invited Zu An, someone so far beneath him, for a meeting, something that he himself had never even dared to dream of!

They are confidantes!

The word appeared in his mind, but he immediately realized that it didn’t accurately reflect what he was thinking about.

Then again, Zu An was about to enter the carriage and share the same cabin as her. To a certain degree, it was true.

As he looked at Zu An, his eyes were no longer filled with endless contempt, but unfathomable jealousy.

Why? Why did the gorgeous Chu First Miss choose him?!

Why is the number one beauty of the capital, Yu Yanluo, also treating him like this?

You have successfully trolled Zhao Zhi for 555… 555… 555…

Worst of all, Yu Yanluo had seen him in this sorry state. What a total humiliation!

He’d consoled himself that not many had been around to witness these events, especially since all the commoners had run off. Chu Youzhao and Murong Qinghe were both part of King Qi’s faction, and they would probably not speak of this. The carriage seemed to belong to a common clan, and all he had to do was to secretly get rid of them.

That way, no news about his utter defeat to Zu An would leak out. However, the number one beauty of the capital, Yu Yanluo, just had to be inside that seemingly ordinary carriage!

There was no way he could kill her. His dad would probably be the first to beat him to death if he even dared to try.

Even he himself couldn’t bear to do it.

He’d seen her from a distance before, and even dreamed about her. That exceptional face seemed to be something that didn’t belong to this mortal world. Which man could steel their resolve enough to destroy such a natural work of art?

The more he ruminated, the more unbearable the memory of being crushed by Zu An became.

It’s all because of that son of a bitch Zu An!

You have successfully trolled Zhao Zhi for 1024… 1024… 1024…

Zu An gave the driver a look of surprise. “Who is this Madam of yours?”

The driver with the missing tooth chuckled. “You’ll know once you enter the carriage.”

Zu An glanced at King Qi’s heir, then at Han Fengqiu, feeling slightly puzzled. Who could make both of them react like this?

The woman inside this carriage spoke up. “Young heir, this young master saved my life earlier on. Could I ask you humbly to let this matter rest, for my sake?”

The voice was soft and pleasant. It was neither cute nor childish, yet it was enough to soften the hearts of all who heard it.

Murong Qinghe was extremely envious. “I wish my voice sounded that good.”

“Your voice is pretty nice. It’s a lot nicer than a certain pretentious person,” said Chu Youzhao with a snort. For some reason, she felt a natural enmity towards this mysterious woman. She was clearly up to no good.

“Really?” Murong Qinghe was extremely happy to hear her crush praise her in such a manner, and her cheeks turned slightly rosy.

My voice obviously doesn’t sound as good, but big brother Chu still said such a thing. He must care a lot about me. Ah! I’m so embarrassed.

King Qi’s heir felt his heart tremble as well. Yu Yanluo actually spoke to me! And she took the initiative to do so!

He was a bit overwhelmed and subconsciously tried to stand up and sort out his clothes. Only then did he notice that all of his clothes had been blasted to ashes by his own lightning. Fortunately, Han Fengqiu acted quickly and covered him up to hide this ugly sight.

He gave Zu An a hateful glare. I will get my revenge, or my name is not Zhao Zhi!

When he looked in the direction of the carriage, though, his malicious expression was instantly replaced by a smile. “Madam, since you are the one making this request, I must show you the appropriate respect. Mister Zu, your murders of military officers will be properly investigated. We’re leaving!”

Han Fengqiu sighed in relief. He had no idea what they would do if they stayed to confront this mysterious expert.

This young heir was worthy of his position after all, taking this opportunity to withdraw without making it look like they were fleeing.

However, the young heir he was praising was full of regret. Bloody hell, why the hell am I trying to act cool?

If he had stubbornly refused her and stayed instead, he might have invited more of the Madam’s attention to himself. At the very least, he could have exchanged a few more words with her.

He sighed. He’d finally landed such a rare opportunity to talk to her, yet he couldn’t even meet her face to face. Instead, Zu An was the one given the chance to interact so closely with her inside the carriage.

This Zu An is only a spineless coward who only knows how to hide behind a woman!

Then again, I want to hide behind a woman like Madam Yu too.

Ahh!! This is so infuriating!

You have successfully trolled Zhao Zhi for 924… 924… 924…

Zu An watched them as they left. After a slight moment of indecision, he decided not to do anything.

It wasn’t easy to deal with someone at the peak of the ninth rank, and the brat’s dad, King Qi, was an even bigger problem. It wasn’t the right time to go all in.

“Excuse me. Our Madam has invited you to meet her.” The driver walked up to him, his tone slightly dissatisfied.

What the heck is this? Anyone in the capital would be honored to be offered a meeting with my Madam, and would be eager to meet her. Why does this good-for-nothing brat seems so disinterested?

“Sorry, I was lost in my thoughts for a moment,” replied Zu An with a laugh. He snapped out of his daze and strode towards the carriage. He was quite curious as to who was inside.

Chu Youzhao and Murong Qinghe subconsciously tried to follow him to find out who it was, but they were stopped by the driver. “Young miss, young master, please wait here.”[1]

Murong Qinghe wasn’t bothered, but Chu Youzhao was both angry and nervous. Unfortunately, there wasn’t much she could do against someone who was at least at the master rank. She could only watch as her brother-in-law walked over to the carriage, as if he were walking straight into a succubus’ lair.

When Zu An arrived next to the carriage, he clasped his fist and said, “Thank you for your help, Madam.”

A chuckle sounded from within. “You saved me earlier on as well.”

Chu Youzhao’s nose wrinkled in disdain. This woman’s laughter was just like that of a witch! Wasn’t she clearly trying to seduce him? She had to admit, though, that it was the most beautiful voice she had ever heard.

Zu An laughed in a self-deprecating manner. “If I had known that the madam had such a formidable guard, I wouldn’t have meddled. I was almost done in by those fellows from King Qi’s Estate.”

As if sensing his dissatisfaction, the woman inside said with a gentle voice, “This only serves as further proof of your valor. Even though outsiders might think of you as an uncouth and frivolous man, I know that there is a chivalrous core deep within you. Regardless of how powerful the enemy is, you will always stand against them when you see injustice. This was how it was back then, and it is still the same now.”

Zu An’s cheeks warmed. “I’m starting to get embarrassed from all this praise, haha… Hm? It seems like you know me?”

There was a momentary silence within the carriage, after which the woman inside said, “You should come inside.”

The driver with the missing tooth was stunned. Did I hear it wrongly? Is Madam actually inviting him inside?

The two of them were going to share that narrow space?

He had worked as a driver for this particular Madam for many years. He knew that, because of her stunning beauty, she cared a lot about the distance between her and those she interacted with. She never let any other man get that close to her.

Now, though, she was inviting this brat inside! She seemed to care a lot about Zu An. He began to wonder if she had secretly fallen for him.

This brat is pretty decent-looking, but is this Madam of mine someone who cares about appearance? She won’t ever find someone as pretty as the one she sees in the mirror, so appearance probably doesn’t matter to her.

Maybe she admires him as an impressive junior? Yeah, that’s possible…

As all manner of thoughts ran through the driver’s mind, Zu An moved aside the curtains and went into the carriage. Before he could lay eyes on the woman, though, a wave of dizziness overcame him.

Bloody hell, is this the rebound of the ‘Keyboard, Come!’ ability? How is it this brutal?

As this thought emerged in his mind, he lost control of his body, and fell involuntarily. There came a woman’s cry of alarm, and he collapsed into a soft body.

1. Editor’s note: The author’s original text translates as “two young misses”, which I believe is an error. The driver shouldn’t know that Youzhao is actually a woman masquerading as a man. If not, Murong Qinghe would have reacted very differently.

chapters.title: Chapter 694: Auntie, I Don’T Want To Work Anymore
chapters.body:
Chapter 694: Auntie, I Don’T Want To Work Anymore

The driver with the missing tooth had been keeping a vigilant watch. When he heard the woman cry out in alarm, his expression flickered, and he was instantly beside the carriage. “Madam, what happened?”

He didn’t dare go inside, since he knew she was rather reclusive, and he wasn’t allowed inside her private space unless given explicit permission.

Chu Youzhao and Murong Qinghe rushed over as well, but there was no way they could keep up with the driver, and arrived slightly behind him.

Murong Qinghe tugged on Chu Youzhao’s sleeves, looking at her with a strange expression. “Big brother Chu, your brother-in-law really is…”

Her face turned red, and she couldn’t bring herself to continue.

Chu Youzhao also stomped her feet in annoyance. “Who are you calling my brother-in-law? I don’t have such a perverted brother-in-law!”

You have successfully trolled Chu Youzhao for 223… 223… 223…

Murong Qinghe wrinkled her nose. Who was the one who had kept saying ‘brother-in-law’ earlier?

The Madam’s voice came once again from within the carriage. “Nothing… It’s nothing. Uncle Fu, please stand guard. Do not let anyone approach.”

The driver with the missing tooth was still uncertain. “Madam, are you sure there’s nothing wrong?”

He was afraid that she was only saying this because she was being threatened.

The Madam’s voice sounded again. “Don’t worry, I’m fine. I have some things I wish to talk to him about. Please stand guard outside.”

A layer of blue light appeared around the carriage. The driver knew that a restriction that cut off all sound was activated.

Madam was the only one who knew about this. This wouldn’t have been possible unless she was the one who activated it.

This thought made him sigh in relief. “This old servant will wait outside.”

He shooed Chu Youzhao and Murong Qinghe away as he said this.

He couldn’t help but feel a sense of shock that this Madam of his had actually activated this restriction. Apparently, she really did trust this brat. If anything dangerous occurred within, no one would be able to save her even if she screamed her lungs out.

Chu Youzhao was pushed a great distance away by an invisible force, and was visibly upset. “What is your Madam doing, locking away my brother-in-law? Are you plotting against him?”

“Madam? Plotting against your brother-in-law?” The driver was so irate that he almost laughed. If he lined up all the men who were willing to scheme against Madam in the capital, the line might be able to go around the capital three times, starting from the palace gates. Who’s scared of who?

As they bickered with each other, a stunning, mature woman was reclining on a soft seat within, her enchanting, star-filled eyes looking upon the man lying on her. She wore an ambiguous smile. “How much longer are you planning to lie there for?”

Zu An was extremely distressed. Am I the type of shameless bastard to take advantage of a woman? I’ve already completely distinguished myself from such lowly people. I relied on my own charm to attract beauties, who come to me of their own accord!

What the heck is this, pushing her down as soon as I enter? She’s going to think that I’m a pervert! I still have some dignity, you know?

Despite all this, he never expected the rebound of ‘Keyboard, Come!’ to be so great.

It was different from before. Previously, he had used ‘Keyboard, Come!’ to create a new set of golden token envoy uniforms, so he had expected the rebound.

This time, though, he’d only made that old man’s pants drop. Why was the rebound still so great?

However, he had miscalculated. The pants of a powerful ninth rank expert would never drop for no reason. Any expert of a similar level would be able to use that momentary distraction to take Han Fengqiu’s life. Despite the seemingly-minute action of causing his pants to fall, in reality, it was related to the very survival of a cultivator at the peak of the ninth rank. How could the rebound from affecting such a thing be small?

Fortunately, this was only an indirect effect. If not, Zu An’s life might have been in danger.

Zu An forced a smile. “Would you believe me if I said that my strength gave out because I just fought a great battle, and that I wasn’t trying to take advantage of you?”

He thought that he was done for, but the response he received was unexpected. “I do. Despite your youth, you were able to fight an eighth rank cultivator, and someone at the peak of ninth rank. It’s impressive you survived this long.”

Zu An: couldn’t believe what he’d heard.

Even I wouldn’t believe what I said if I was on the receiving end. Is this woman dumb?

Huh? Wait, why does she look familiar?

A hint of impatience finally crept into the beautiful woman’s voice. “Are you still not going to get up?”

“My bad, my bad.” Zu An laughed awkwardly and quickly sat up.

He wasn’t a naive or innocent kid anymore, but the astonishingly soft feeling even caused his heart to start pounding.

The beautiful woman straightened as well, and sorted out her clothes and hair.

Zu An finally got a chance to get a good look at her. Her skin was fair and smooth, and her face was flawless. Her hair was coiled above her head in an elegant fashion, making her neck appear graceful and slender.

A luminescent pearl was embedded at the top of the carriage. The soft radiance illuminated her cheeks, causing her gentle features to seem to have been painted by a master’s brush. It was difficult to say who was brighter and more beautiful, the luminescent pearl, or this woman..

“It’s you!” Zu An finally recognized who this was, and was both startled and happy. The woman in front of him was precisely the one he and Ji Xiaoxi had shared back then, the capital’s number one beauty: Yu Yanluo!

“So, we meet again.” Yu Yanluo smiled her beautiful smile, blossoming like a brilliant flower in bloom.

She showed no anger at being offended, nor was there any trace of a young lady’s shyness. Her demeanor was natural and calm, as though she was the main character in this world.

“It’s been a long time, Madam, yet you are as stunning as the last time I saw you, if not more so.” Zu An sighed. “I was wondering why King Qi’s heir suddenly turned into such an obsequious flatterer. Now that I’ve met you again, Madam, I finally understand.”

Yu Yanluo showed little reaction to his praise. After all, she’d been subjected to much more than that over the years. “Your growth is quite surprising. The last time I met you, those Blackwing Stockade scoundrels were giving you a hard time, yet now, a few months later, you’re already going toe-to-toe with experts at the peak of the ninth rank.”

Zu An chuckled. “I remember you telling me the last time that I still had a few years to go before I was worthy of being your man. Now that you’ve seen how much I’ve grown, has that changed your mind?”

Yu Yanluo finally lost some of her composure, a faint blush appearing on her gorgeous face. She gave him a rebuking look. “What am I going to do with you…”

Zu An had to admit that someone so beautiful was lovely to look at, even when she was angry. However, for better or for worse, his tolerance towards beauty had been raised after all the girls he had met. He quickly recovered his own composure and said in an upset manner, “It’s your fault for forgetting your past promise!”

“Me, forget my promise?” Yu Yanluo stretched her palm out. Her fingers were fine and slender, and seemed as though they had been sculpted from fine jade. “Return it to me.”

“Return what?” Zu An was stunned.

“My jade pendant.” Yu Yanluo snorted. “I don’t want you to use it to spread rumors everywhere, especially not that it’s some token of love given to you because I’d fallen for you.”

No matter how thick Zu An’s skin was, he was still extremely ashamed that his empty boasting had been exposed.

However, he reacted quickly as well, and went on the offensive. “You really do forget the debts you owe! I saved your life previously, and you gave me this pendant, promising that you were going to repay me. However, when I visited the Yu Estate in Brightmoon City, you were nowhere to be seen! Now that I’ve finally met you again, you’re demanding the pendant back?”

Yu Yanluo explained herself. “Too much was happening in Brightmoon City back then, so I had no choice but to leave. That’s why I didn’t have a chance to thank you properly. However, I found a way to compensate you later on.”

Zu An was confused. “You’ve compensated me?”

A faint smile shone in Yu Yanluo’s eyes. “Why else do you think His Majesty let you live?

…

Meanwhile, in King Qi’s Estate, King Qi stood over his lightning-scorched son, his expression as dark as a thundercloud. “Who the hell did this?!”

chapters.title: Chapter 695, Part I: Chess Pieces, And The Player Who Moves Them
chapters.body:
Chapter 695, Part I: Chess Pieces, And The Player Who Moves Them

King Qi’s heir opened his mouth to speak. He wanted to hug his dad and cry to him to take revenge, but he suddenly remembered the vow he’d made with Zu An. He couldn’t ask his father to help. As such, he resisted this urge.

Besides, given his father’s nature, tearfully complaining to him might just achieve the opposite effect.

When Han Fengqiu saw him holding back his words, he spoke up, out of consideration for him. “It was that Zu An brat.”

“Zu An?” King Qi frowned. “Are you kidding me? He only has six to seven ranks of cultivation. How was he able to hurt you this badly?”

The young heir’s face went entirely red. He was also incredibly baffled by this. He wanted to understand how he had managed to lose so horribly to that brat, despite his own eight ranks of cultivation.

Han Fengqiu offered an explanation. “That fellow’s movement technique is rather bizarre, and not even I could catch him. The young master was surely at a disadvantage.”

“Not even you could catch him?” King Qi was surprised to hear this. Even if he discounted his own son’s strength, he was very familiar with how strong Han Fengqiu was. He was well-stocked in both cultivation and combat experience. If even he couldn’t catch Zu An, then what sort of demon did that make him?

Han Fengqiu felt his cheeks grow warm. He quickly added, “It’s all because of the restrictions regarding flight around the capital. Without them, I would have caught him no matter how fast he is. Even so, I almost took him down, but Madam Yu’s subordinate, Uncle Fu, interfered. I wasn’t able to do any more than bring the young heir back here.”

“Madam Yu?” King Qi’s breathing became hurried. “Are you talking about Yu Yanluo?”

“Yes, it was none other than her,” replied Han Fengqiu.

King Qi’s heir saw his father’s composure slip momentarily, and knew that he should have been outraged for his mother’s sake. However, he knew that Yu Yanluo had thrown the entire capital into disarray before. As a man, he actually sympathized with his father.

“She’s actually returned to the capital?” King Qi felt a twinge of excitement. He began to pace around the room, finally stopping at a window and looking out into the distance. The fire in his eyes gradually simmered down. “It’s a pity, unfortunately. Ten years ago, I would have disregarded everything else and rushed over to meet with her, but I am no longer in the mood.”

“It is a blessing for us subjects if the ruler cares more about the greater situation,” said Han Fengqiu obsequiously. 

King Qi turned around. “Why would Yu Yanluo save Zu An?”

Han Fengqiu shook his head. He didn’t know why either.

The young heir spoke up, his voice quivering. “Perhaps it was because Zu An saved their lives…”

He told his father about the startled horse.

King Qi nodded. “This plan of yours was rather good, and you used the law to your advantage in every move. You didn’t let my teachings go to waste.”

Zhao Zhi was overjoyed. “Thank you, father!”

He wasn’t King Qi’s only son, so he was also under immense pressure. He obviously wanted to increase the gulf between himself and the other potential heirs, to prevent them from having any thoughts they shouldn’t have.

Where did his advantage come from? Obviously, it was from his father doting on him.

Unexpectedly, King Qi gave a cold snort. “Unfortunately, this plan wasn’t perfect. Since it was supposed to be a trap, why didn’t you arrange your own carriage?”

“I was afraid it would be too obvious,” explained Zhao Zhi, “The Liu, Meng, and Bi clans within the imperial court would surely make a fuss over it, so I decided to target a passing carriage instead. We carefully observed it beforehand. That carriage was completely ordinary. How could we have known that it would be Yu Yanluo's carriage?”

“Hmph, a slight bit of negligence can ruin everything,” King Qi said, reprimanding him. “So what if anyone in the imperial court suspects you? Once irrefutable evidence is brought forward, they will do no more than bark a couple of times. Because of your uncertainty, though, the entire situation was left in ruins. You saved a little, only to lose a lot. Remember, you have to be clear on what kind of risks you can shoulder, and which risks you cannot afford to take.”

“Thank you for your advice, Father!” Zhao Zhi rose to offer a respectful bow. Unfortunately, the pain in his knees immediately made sweat trickle down his face.

“Your knees are crushed?” Only now did King Qi begin to examine his injuries. As he did so, his expression darkened.

“I am an incompetent child. I’ve let you down, Father.” Zhao Zhi knew that his father hated excuses the most. It was better to just humble himself and admit his inadequacy.

Sure enough, some tension drained out of King Qi’s expression when he heard this. He reached out his hand and gently caressed his kneecaps. Two white lights appeared in his palms.

Zhao Zhi’s brows knitted together in pain, but in moments, his expression gradually eased. “Thank you, Father!”

Han Fengqiu sighed in admiration. As expected, his master’s cultivation was deep and profound, and he was able to heal such serious injuries so easily. I took a look at it myself earlier on, yet I couldn’t do anything about it.

“I’ve only prevented any permanent damage. You still require the physicians to treat you. It will take a few months for you to make a full recovery.” King Qi’s voice was cold. “No further harm will come from this. Think on your mistakes, and do not disgrace yourself further.”

Zhao Zhi seemed slightly dissatisfied by this, but he did not dare to retort. “Understood!”

His hatred of Zu An was already at its absolute limit. Once he recovered, he was going to chop that fellow’s corpse into ten thousand pieces!

You have successfully trolled Zhao Zhi for 999… 999… 999…

“Now that that’s taken care of, tell me the details of your battle against Zu An.” King Qi smoothed his clothing out and sat down, his posture perfectly upright.

He was perplexed by Zu An’s apparent combat strength. Was it because of the Phoenix Nirvana Sutra? If it really was so incredible, didn’t that mean that, if His Majesty cultivated it…?

Zhao Zhi endured the pain and did his best to sit up. Then, he told his father about the battle.

King Qi listened, analyzing what happened. “Zu An’s strange movement technique should be Eunuch Mi’s Sunflower Phantasm. Zu An was probably designated as his inheritor, and he most likely passed it onto him while in Brightmoon City. However, even though the Sunflower Phantasm has its strong points, it shouldn’t be performing at this level.”

Han Fengqiu spoke. “My King, now that you mention it, I too sense that the youngster’s movement skill is similar to the rumored Sunflower Phantasm. However, something was different about it, although even I cannot put my finger on what it is.”

King Qi nodded. He looked back at his son, his tone growing slightly displeased. “Even with a strange movement technique to aid him, you still have eight ranks of cultivation. How did you get beaten so miserably?”

Zhao Zhi’s face warmed. He quickly said, “I held the advantage throughout the entire fight. However, because of his strange movement technique, I was sick of playing hide-and-seek, so I used Holy Lightning of the Hegemon. I wanted to trap him within a sea of lightning.”

Han Fengqiu nodded. He had chosen to fill the sky with swords because he had similar thoughts. An area-of-effect skill was needed to deal with such a strange movement technique.

“And then?” King Qi was unmoved. His son’s wretched state told him that it had been the wrong choice.

Zhao Zhi’s voice was full of grief and indignation. “A giant ball of water suddenly appeared and soaked me through, so all of the thunder clouds attacked me instead! Once I was completely paralyzed, that bastard Zu An… he…”

His mood fell even further when he thought about how he’d been stepped on, and then had his knees crushed. His eyes couldn’t help but begin to water.

King Qi stared at him. When he saw that his son didn’t cry, he nodded in satisfaction. “You’ve had too easy of a life so far. This is a good lesson for you.”

Zhao Zhi didn’t dare retort. He only replied, “Yes!”

Han Fengqiu quickly spoke up for the young heir. “This means that there should’ve been a water element expert hiding nearby, stepping in and acting against the young master at the crucial moment. If not for this, the young heir would have defeated Zu An.”

“A water element expert?” After turning it over in his mind, King Qi accepted this conclusion. Zu An had apparently awakened the ice element, so he shouldn’t have been able to use the water element as well. There had to be a water element cultivator around. However, who could it be?

He began to run through all of the water element experts in the capital. Those who would dare raise a hand against Zhao Zhi had to be above Han Fengqiu’s level. Several candidates appeared, but none of them seemed too likely. However, after further consideration, it seemed to him that every single one of them might have had a motive. His brow furrowed deeply.

“Once I recover, I will definitely skin Zu An alive!” spat Zhao Zhi hatefully. “Only then can I satisfy this hatred.”

After listening to their analysis, he too realized that, no matter how formidable Zu An was, only a water element user could have saved him in that situation. Zu An himself only relied on his strange movement technique. In a fair fight, the brat was no match for him.

King Qi gave a fierce snort. “Have you not brought enough shame to yourself? You should focus on your recovery. Once you recover, you’re not allowed to go anywhere. Do not forget that it was your idiotic self that took that absurd oath!”

Zhao Zhi began to panic. “Won’t it be fine as long as I do not rely on the backing of our clan? I’ll be more prepared next time! I can easily take Zu An down by myself.”

King Qi gave him a disappointed look. “Do you think that your actions only reflect on yourself? Your father is facing a crucial moment, and the entire court’s eyes are on me. I’ve always maintained a clean reputation, so that there is nothing for them to use against me. If you cause a ruckus, won’t we be handing them a weapon to aim at us?”

Zhao Zhi now understood his father’s misgivings, but even so, he still felt dissatisfied. “How can we leave the matter like this? He broke both of my legs! If he doesn’t pay the price, no one will take our clan seriously. Won’t that bring us more trouble? Shaming me isn’t a big deal, but this humiliates you as well!”

King Qi sneered. “Don’t try to use your petty tricks on me. Did I say I was going to let him go?”

chapters.title: Chapter 695, Part Ii: Chess Pieces, And The Player Who Moves Them
chapters.body:
Chapter 695, Part Ii: Chess Pieces, And The Player Who Moves Them

Zhao Zhi’s eyes lit up. “What kind of magnificent move do you have in mind, Father?”

King Qi snorted. When he stood up again, there was a mysterious grandeur to his presence. “I will teach you one more lesson today. More often than not, it is better not to charge forward rashly. Even the strongest and smartest person is nothing more than a chess piece. You have to leap out of the chessboard and become the chess player.”

“The chess player?” Zhao Zhi was taken aback, and began to ponder over this.

King Qi laughed and said, “Why do you need to make a move yourself if you want to take care of Zu An? There are others who want him dead more than you do. As long as you use that chess piece well, wouldn’t it be far better than doing the deed yourself?”

Zhao Zhi’s eyes lit up. “Are you talking about the Shi clan?”

He had reached the eighth rank before the age of forty. Even though he had had the resources of King Qi’s Estate behind him, it was still a reflection of his own talent and intelligence. He wasn’t stupid.

King Qi nodded. “Shi Kun lost his life a few days ago in the Eastern Palace. It’s rumored that the crown princess accidentally wounded him, so the Shi clan had to keep a handle on their rage. Even if it had been intentional, they would have been powerless to act. However, just because they cannot get their revenge on the crown princess doesn’t mean that they cannot take their anger out on anyone else. Zu An was the reason why Shi Kun met his fate. The  Shi clan hasn’t made a move yet, either because they haven’t realized this, or they may still have some misgivings. All you need to do is give them a push.”

Zhao Zhi grew excited. “As expected, Father, your plan is brilliant! Not only will this force the Shi clan to target Zu An, we can even drag the Shi clan, who is currently neutral, into our camp. After all, Shi Kun was the son that the Minister of War doted on the most. He died at the crown princess’ hands. If the crown prince rises to the throne, he will never be able to have his revenge. Only by cooperating with us will he have that opportunity.”

King Qi finally smiled. “Indeed. You haven’t let me down.”

Zhao Zhi replied to his father humbly, but his expression was vicious. “Zu An, let’s see what you’ll do now!”

The best way to take revenge on a man was to beat him up, then seize his women and make him hear them weeping.

Hmph, I need to find a chance to get my hands on Chu Chuyan, and then I’ll toy with her right in front of him. I want to see just what sort of expression he will show me when that happens.

…

“Achoo!” Inside the carriage, Zu An sneezed. He subconsciously rubbed his nose. “Hm? It seems quite a few people are thinking about me.”

Yu Yanluo covered her mouth and said with a smile, “I reckon they probably want you dead. You’re quite good at making enemies.”

Zu An shrugged his shoulders. “What can I say? I’m too outstanding. As the saying goes, the tallest tree in the forest is the one that gets blown over.”

Yu Yanluo was speechless.

How could this guy make shamelessness seem so refined and justified?

Zu An put away his smile. “At least now I know that His Majesty let me live because of you. Thank you, Madam.”

He’d still felt lingering fears over the matter. He assumed that it had all been because of the Hat of Forgiveness, but it was actually because of her help.

No wonder the emperor said that someone had spoken up for him. Back then, he’d suspected that it might have been Qin Wanru.

“You saved my life outside Brightmoon City. I was only repaying a debt.” Yu Yanluo said with an ambiguous smile. “I hope that no one continues to harbor the misconception that I’m someone who doesn’t repay their debts.”

Zu An laughed in embarrassment. He remembered criticizing her about that just a moment ago. He quickly changed the topic and said, “By the way, you have such a formidable bodyguard with you this time. How were you almost done in by the Blackwind Stockade previously?”

Yu Yanluo sighed, her eyes filled with great distress. “Too many things happened back then…”

Zu An was anticipating a long and complex story, but she didn’t say anything more than that.

In a moment, Yu Yanluo snapped out of her daze. “Sorry, I’ve shown you a poor side of myself.”

Zu An said with a chuckle, “Who knows how many men in this world wish to see this side of you, Madam, but will never be able to. Any smile on my face will be one of happiness. Why would I ever mock you?”

Yu Yanluo couldn’t help but laugh. “Your tongue is formidable after all.” 

Zu An let his eyes rest on hers. “Even though I do not know why you are worried, Madam, all things will pass, and there will always be people who will show you understanding. I wish you peace.”

“All things will pass…” Yu Yanluo muttered to herself. Her eyes lit up. “I thought that you only knew how to tease others, but you’re pretty good at consoling them as well. These so-called people who will understand, are you talking about yourself?”

Zu An shook his head. “I know myself, at the very least. I fear that I do not have the right to discuss these things with you now. As for the future, that’s hard to say.”

“Didn’t you say I was too old for you?” Yu Yanluo asked with a grin, which seemed to harbor some ambiguity.

Zu An’s cheeks warmed. “I only said that to trick that brat Zhao Zhi, so please don’t take that to heart, Madam. You’re so beautiful that it isn’t absurd for someone to believe that you’re actually my little sister.”

Yu Yanluo’s face reddened. Everyone else behaved properly when they met her, fearful of leaving her with a bad impression of them, yet this fellow was always so natural with his jokes that she didn’t find them that offensive.

She snorted. “You’re only good at joking around. Return that pendant, please. I don’t want to hear any malicious rumors flying around anymore.”

Zu An took out the jade pendant and handed it to her. Her hand was so much more beautiful than that intricate piece of jade.

Yu Yanluo was surprised. She had expected him not to return it to her, but he’d actually given it up right away.

Her admiration for him went up a notch. “Now that you’ve offended King Qi’s heir, what are you going to do?”

Zu An smiled. “Don’t worry. They won’t be able to do anything to me for quite some time.”

Ever since the moment King Qi’s faction sent assassins after him while he was being transported to the capital, there was no chance of the two being on the same side. With the missions that the emperor had entrusted him with, and then the friendships he made in the Eastern Palace, there was no way that he would get along with King Qi. To put it bluntly, with these powers backing him, it wouldn’t be easy for King Qi to touch him.

Yu Yanluo said, “If anyone else had told me this, I might have thought that they were arrogant, but you’re different. Ever since I met you, you seem to be creating miracles, one after another. I believe that you will turn misfortunes into blessings. If you are truly faced with an insurmountable obstacle, you can seek me out. Even though I might not be able to protect you, I can still offer you some help.”

“Thank you, Madam!” Zu An was overjoyed. Her status, as well as the resources at her disposal, made her a great ally. This promise was basically a get-out-of-jail-free card.

After chatting a while longer, Yu Yanluo bid him farewell. She’d made this trip back to the capital in secret, so she didn’t wish to linger.

When Zu An left the carriage, the driver with the missing tooth sized him up with a strange expression, then sniffed him. He didn’t detect anything strange, and relaxed. “Kid, you’re pretty good. I have high expectations for you. Hahaha…”

With a flick of his whip, the carriage moved off.

“What the heck is up with that driver? Why is his smile so perverted?” Chu Youzhao ran over. She was clearly unhappy that she wasn’t allowed to get closer earlier. “Brother-in-law, are you okay? Did that vixen… ahem, did anything happen between the two of you?”

“What could have possibly happened to me?” Zu An was rather confused by her question.

Chu Youzhao’s face turned red. She quickly said, “Who is that woman in the carriage?”

“She’s a beautiful woman.” Zu An was still in awe of Yu Yanluo’s beauty. Why would the heavens bless a single woman with so much? It was almost as if it did everything to make her the most perfect beauty possible.

Hm, this woman is a perfect match for me.

Chu Youzhao grew worried when he didn’t say anything else. “Come on, who was she?”

Murong Qinghe’s eyes widened as well. She too was extremely curious. Just what kind of woman could make such a powerful expert willingly serve as her driver?

Zu An was getting annoyed. “Why are you kids asking about all of this? She’s a special person, so I can’t just reveal her identity like that.”

“You can’t even tell me?” Chu Youzhao grinded her teeth.

Zu An rolled his eyes. “You’re not even my wife.”

“You!” Chu Youzhao wanted to bite him.

Murong Qinghe was confused. Is this fellow into men? My big brother Chu is so pretty, I need to protect him!

A sudden rumbling caught their attention. The three of them turned around and saw a large contingent of horses galloping in their direction.

chapters.title: Chapter 696: The Crown Princess’ Worries
chapters.body:
Chapter 696: The Crown Princess’ Worries

Chu Youzhao immediately rushed over to Zu An’s side. “Brother-in-law, be careful!”

She thought that King Qi's faction was back for revenge. However, Murong Qinghe saw who they were and said quietly, “They’re from the Eastern Palace.”

Chu Youzhao froze, and the horsemen quickly surrounded the three of them.

A carriage came into view behind the horses. A eunuch moved the curtains aside, and a lavishly-dressed woman slowly walked out. Her golden headdress and jade jewelry were dazzling in the sunlight, giving her an elegant and stunning appearance.

“I greet the crown princess!” Chu Youzhao and Murong Qinghe were both individuals who had grown up in the capital, so they naturally recognized Bi Linglong. Even though they belonged to a different faction, they had to admit that this woman was beautiful, and the noble air around her made her truly exceptional. Her lavish outfit merely accentuated her beauty further.

The crown princess nodded slightly towards the two of them. She wasn’t inclined towards those from King Qi’s faction, so she only showed them the most basic courtesy.

Then, she walked towards Zu An, casting her gaze over him. When she saw that he’d suffered nothing major, she sighed in relief. “I heard that something happened to you, Lord Zu, and I happened to be in the area. They didn’t do much to you, did they?”

As she spoke, she directed a puzzled look at Chu Youzhao and Murong Qinghe. Wasn’t King Qi’s faction being a little too careless, sending these two to deal with Zu An?

Zu An said quickly, “You’re mistaken, Crown Princess. They are my friends.”

“Friends?” The crown princess raised her beautiful brows. This fellow is actually friends with these people from King Qi’s faction? Interesting…

Murong Qinghe sighed in relief. “We still have things to take care of back in our clans. We will take our leave.”

The Murong clan had opposed the crown prince from the start, so she didn’t feel comfortable staying here.

She also dragged Chu Youzhao with her. Chu Youzhao was unwilling to leave, but she knew that she couldn’t really say anything with the crown princess around. Furthermore, she’d lived in the Qin Estate for a long time, and their relationship with the Eastern Palace had never been good. Being surrounded by the Eastern Palace’s people was rather uncomfortable. As she left, she gave Zu An a look, as though she’d been wronged.

The crown princess had no reason to keep them. As she watched their retreating figures, she couldn’t help but say, “It looks like your relationship with your little brother-in-law isn’t that bad.”

Chu Youzhao’s parting expression hadn’t escaped her notice. She was a little surprised. Didn’t the Chu clan chase Zu An out at the crucial moment? Why were the two sides still on such good terms with each other?

Zu An smiled. “Apart from being handsome, another of my virtues is that I get along with people easily. People who hang around me for a while can’t help but like me.”

The crown princess had no reply.

Why did she feel like beating him up whenever she saw his smile?

“If there’s nothing else, let’s head back to the Eastern Palace.” With a sniff, she returned to her carriage.

“Very well.” Even though the crown princess’ words seemed a little harsh to Zu An, he knew she wasn’t a bad person. She’d come over immediately when she learned that something had happened to him. Even though she wasn’t too happy right now, she was still worried that those from King Qi’s faction might take action against him, so she was escorting him back to the palace.

They didn’t say anything to each other along the way. With so many people, there was no way for Zu An to head over to her carriage to talk to her, no matter how good he was with words.

It didn’t take them long to return to the Eastern Palace. Zu An was surprised to discover Piao Duandiao and Jiao Sigun kneeling at the entrance, clearly being punished. “What are you two doing?” he exclaimed. “Who dares treat the guards of the Eastern Palace this way? It’s absolutely preposterous!”

“I was the one who punished them,” said the crown princess, walking up behind him. Piao Duandiao and Jiao Sigun both smiled bitterly towards Zu An, and continued to kneel with their heads down.

“Crown Princess, why did you punish them?” Zu An couldn’t help but ask.

The crown princess looked at them disdainfully. “They dragged you into the government brothel, and didn’t return with you. They even caused you to fall into the trap laid by King Qi’s Estate. Fortunately, you made it through this affair. If not, this would not be the end of their punishment.”

Piao Duandiao and Jiao Sigun were slightly aggrieved. It was clearly Zu An who had brought them to the government brothel. When did they become the ones who had dragged him along to that place?

However, Zu An had given them a treat, and had fallen into danger as well, so they couldn’t bring themselves to correct her.

Zu An was stunned. He didn’t expect her to care so much about him.

The crown princess saw the intensity in his gaze and froze, her face turning red. “You’re thinking too much. As crown princess, I would do my best to rescue any member of the Eastern Palace if they are being targeted by King Qi’s Estate. It isn’t just you.”

Zu An smiled. “Thank you, Crown Princess. This subordinate will work diligently and conscientiously for you until my death.”

Piao Duandiao and Jiao Sigun felt their eyes widen. Look at how good he is at flattery! He is truly worthy of being our big brother.

The crown princess’ eyes lit up. She never expected him to borrow a line from classical literature. Her respect for him rose a notch. As expected of the man who had caused both King Qi and his son to suffer in succession.

Wasn’t this kid a little too brazen, though? How dare he stare at her like this?

Hmph, if I avert my gaze now, wouldn’t that show that I’m scared of him? How will I control him in the future?

What a joke!

She decided not to avert her gaze, but to wait for him to do so. However, Zu An was as shameless as a person could get, and had no such intention.

The two of them continued to stare at each other. The crown princess finally couldn’t take it anymore, and a faint hint of red slowly spread across her fair neck.

The people around them wore weird expressions. Why did it look as though the crown princess and Lord Zu were staring at each other like lovers? How was that possible?

Suddenly, a chubby fellow came over to them. “What’s a government brothel?”

The crown princess finally snapped out of her daze and withdrew her gaze. She gave the crown prince a look of annoyance and said, “It’s a dirty place. You’re not allowed to go.”

“Oh…” He shuddered, frightened by her sudden reaction, causing his fat to jiggle. Clearly, this wife of his scared him.

The crown princess left quickly. She felt her cheeks growing warm. What was up with her today? She’d actually made such a huge mistake. She had to make sure this Zu An got a good beating in the future.

According to the information she received, though, this kid could fight toe-to-toe with King Qi’s heir. He really was a rare genius. The Eastern Palace had been in a long, steady decline. She needed a powerful general like him to face King Qi’s Estate. What could she use to rope him in, though?

When she left, the crown prince quickly pulled Zu An over and asked quietly, “Tell me, tell me. What sort of place is the government brothel?”

Zu An looked at him strangely. “Didn’t the crown princess just tell you?”

“I know she won’t tell me the truth!” The crown prince pouted, as though recalling the two years of oppression he had suffered under her. “Hurry up and tell me! Otherwise, I’ll tell someone about how you were staring at Linglong earlier!”

Zu An was taken aback. Was this fellow really stupid? How had he managed to figure that out? Had he been acting this whole time?

However, he quickly realized that the fact that he could momentarily control the crown prince truly meant that he was stupid. His intellect was about the same as that of a ten-year-old child.

“Ahem, the government brothel is a… a place where people go to exercise.” Zu An had to think hard to describe it tactfully. He did not want to brave the crown princess’ wrath when she found out he had divulged to the crown prince what sort of place it was.

“A place where people exercise?” The crown prince frowned. “You’re lying to me. If it’s just exercise, then it’s definitely no fun, but you and the two guards were there for an entire night.”

As a fatty, he had a natural revulsion towards any form of exercise.

Zu An had a weird look on his face. “Who said that exercising isn’t fun? That place is heaven for men.”

The crown prince immediately grew interested. “So it’s a fun place! You have to bring me next time.”

“Uh…” Zu An felt a headache coming on. He could only say half-heartedly, “Sure, next time, next time.”

The crown prince left, satisfied that he had extracted this promise.

A lesser eunuch came to pass him a message. The emperor had invited Zu An to his study for an audience.

The crown princess watched as Zu An hurried off, surprise evident in her beautiful eyes. The imperial concubines in the palace only met the emperor a few times a year, yet this kid seemed like he was visiting him practically every day.

Such a person is definitely worth investing in. But how am I going to rope him in?

This fella seems to be quite the pervert, so honey traps will probably work. But his wife is a famous beauty! He probably wouldn’t notice an ordinary girl…

Hm… I can’t get personally involved, can I?

…

As the crown princess struggled with her dilemma, Zu An arrived at the imperial study. The emperor was giving off an imposing aura. He looked at Zu An with an expression of foreboding. “This emperor gave you the task of infiltrating the Devil Sect. Have you found any traces of Yun Jianyue yet?”

chapters.title: Chapter 697: Fanning The Flames
chapters.body:
Chapter 697: Fanning The Flames

Zu An felt the emperor’s aura lock onto him, fierce enough to silence him forever if he spoke even a single wrong word. Zu An cursed him in his heart. You’re behaving so arrogantly just because your cultivation is high!

He quickly let his anger subside. A high level of cultivation did indeed make an individual special. At the very least, there was nothing he could do about it right now.

He bowed and said, “Your Majesty. In response to your question, I haven’t yet received any information regarding the Devil Sect’s sect master.”

The emperor gave a grunt of dissatisfaction. “Then what’s the use in sending you to infiltrate the Devil Sect?”

Zu An was rather disgruntled. Wasn’t this guy being too impatient? “Your Majesty, even though this subject did not pick up any news regarding the Devil Sect’s sect master, I have discovered that the Devil Sect has a secret hideout in the capital.”

After some discussion, he and Yun Jianyue had agreed on this course of action. Even though Yun Jianyue didn’t know that he had been given the mission to infiltrate their sect, she didn’t mind helping him out in this manner. After all, from her point of view, Zu An had brought all manner of trouble upon himself for helping her, and this was just a way of giving him an additional layer of protection.

As for that secret hideout, it was obviously a chess piece that had been prepared ahead of time.

“Oh?” The emperor’s expression calmed slightly. “You’ve done well. Lean on your status as an Embroidered Envoy to dig out that hideout.”

Zu An bowed. “Understood!” The emperor was truly a cautious man. He’s making me investigate the location I provided. If it turns out to be empty and deserted, I will only bring suspicion upon myself.

You think you’re already one step ahead, but I’m five steps ahead of you! I’ve already predicted this thought of yours. There are quite a few Devil Sect members there, and many of them are even higher-ranking figures within the sect! Of course, they are all part of factions that oppose Yun Jianyue. She clearly wouldn’t mind using the emperor’s forces to wipe them out once and for all.

These people are all old foxes, I tell ya!

Even though Zu An was mocking the emperor, he was also praising Yun Jianyue. She may look like a goddess, descended from the clouds, yet her schemes were extremely vicious.

He really had to be careful around these people, or he’d end up badly burned.

The emperor nodded in satisfaction, then pretended to mention idly, “By the way, I heard that you got into a dispute with King Qi’s heir. Do you know how grave a crime it is to offend an important member of the court?!”

“This subject has been wrongly accused of this!” Zu An replied quickly. “I was not out to offend him. Rather, he was the one who framed me.”

He explained the scheme that King Qi’s heir had concocted to the emperor.

“Zhao Zhi seems to have inherited some of his father’s intellect. Unfortunately, he is still a little too young,” sneered the emperor. When he looked at Zu An, though, his eyes burned with praise. “I didn’t expect you to see through all of this. Not only that, you even made him suffer for it. You’re rather quick-witted, kid.”

Zu An could sense that the emperor was trying his best not to laugh. The emperor was surely reveling in this victory. After all, he had waged a long, difficult battle with King Qi, and it had been a long while since King Qi’s faction had suffered such a huge loss.

He had to seize the opportunity while the emperor was in a good mood. He adopted a bitter expression and said, “Now, though, I’ve completely offended King Qi. I will be a dead man if King Qi seeks revenge.”

“Don’t worry. Knowing King Qi, he won’t act directly against you, but make use of someone else instead. If you can deal with both Zhao Zhi and Han Fengqiu, who else can do anything to you?” The emperor seemed extremely pleased. He had indeed underestimated this little fellow! Judging from the way he was maturing, he could still be a great asset even if he failed to get the fake manual into King Qi’s hands.

The brat had truly been a thorn in King Qi’s side these past few days.

Zu An sighed in relief. Although the emperor made no promises, he knew that he would never let King Qi act against him directly.

He wasn’t taking such a strong stance against King Qi’s faction because he’d been forced to. He was also doing it to prove himself in the eyes of the emperor.

Only by demonstrating his ability, and showing that he could never live under the same sky as King Qi, would this man be willing to completely trust him and protect him, rather than use him as a mere sacrificial pawn.
